mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in the federal funds rate compress equity discount rates and raise corporate borrowing costs, directly reducing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no significant negative correlation with rate hikes.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate crossing above 4.5 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions and rising recession probability, triggering immediate equity sell-offs.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of triggered instances, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent monthly increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer demand expansion, driving corporate revenue growth and positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in both series fail to predict positive 60-day returns with an accuracy exceeding 58 percent over a 5-year walk-forward evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month rebound in the index of industrial production following a supply shock reversal boosts short-term market sentiment and generates positive one-week returns.
failure_condition: The signal yields negative 5-day forward returns in more than 60 percent of post-shock recovery periods, or Rank IC falls below -0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding the long-term trend rate validates macroeconomic stability and supports elevated corporate earnings forecasts, lifting equity valuations over three months.
failure_condition: GDP revisions above trend consistently coincide with negative 60-day forward returns, or the predictive accuracy drops below 50 percent across multiple expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index year-over-year growth surpassing 3.5 percent forces equity markets to price in aggressive monetary tightening, compressing valuation multiples and depressing forward returns.
failure_condition: Inflation readings above the threshold fail to predict negative 20-day returns, with positive outcomes occurring in more than 55 percent of cases during high-inflation episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate indicate a tightening labor market that sustains wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal produces negative 20-day returns in more than 60 percent of observations, or interaction effects show no statistical improvement over single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts during late-cycle expansions signal a credit-driven demand contraction that disproportionately harms financial and consumer discretionary sectors, dragging down broad market returns.
failure_condition: Housing starts declines in late-cycle regimes fail to predict negative 60-day returns, with positive outcomes exceeding 55 percent of triggered instances.
